title: Define an append-only mutation log and approval-audit surface
summary: Capture self-evolution mutations and approval-state transitions in an explicit append-only audit trail before attempting heavier approval enforcement.
humanSummary: Proposal to add a small append-only mutation log plus approval-audit metadata so eclawlution can explain what changed, who approved it, and why, without pretending hard runtime enforcement already exists.
rationale: Recent repo self-evolution reviews keep converging on the same trust gap: approval metadata exists, but there is no durable mutation or approval audit trail yet.
An append-only log is a practical intermediate step between pure classification and full runtime enforcement.
This would improve observability, rollback confidence, and operator trust without requiring secrets, auth changes, or live restart behavior.
risks: Could imply stronger enforcement than the repo actually ships if the docs get ahead of the implementation.
A rushed design could produce noisy logs or unstable schema that later consumers would have to unwind.
If actor/source metadata is underspecified, the audit trail may look precise while still being hard to trust.
rollbackPlan: revert mutation-log and approval-audit surface changes back to the current proposal-and-report-only flow
remove any unused local log artifacts from examples or docs and re-run local checks
validationChecks: Keep the first implementation additive and local-only, with no auth, secrets, external effects, or live restart behavior.
Require explicit timestamp, actor/source, action kind, and rationale fields on every logged entry.
Keep README, plugin contract, security model, and proposal lifecycle docs aligned about what is observable versus enforced.
nextActions: Design the minimum append-only entry schema for mutation and approval events.
Decide whether the first shipped surface should be report-linked JSONL logging, CLI inspection, or proposal-state transition logging.
Review the design with a maintainer before implementing code or claiming stronger enforcement.
